title: My push date-automation doesn't calculate properly
excerpt:

posts.author: Viva_la_Coaching (Operations)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Everyone,
I am new in Monday.

I tried to create an push date-automation, but i experience a problem with the automation.
P.S This is multi level board.
The webinar is at 26 march and i want the automation to calculate the days i am writing in the table, for example when i write -25 days i expect that the automation calculates -25 days which would be 1st March, but the automation calculates at 1 February.
Also when i write 1 day, which is supposed 1 day plus to be added, it still calculates Minus instead increasing.

Here is image of the automation:

image2473×134 14.1 KB

Here is preview of the calculations:

image423×219 11.2 KB

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Viva_la_Coaching, Welcome to the community!

I think this is happening because you’re using a multi-level board. Automations there don’t always behave the same way as they do on standard boards.

For this kind of use case, I’d suggest:

If you’re on Pro or Enterprise, try using Workflows instead of standard automations as they give more control.

Or use a formula column like:
ADD_DAYS({Webinar Date}, {Days})

This will correctly handle both positive and negative values.

Hope that helps!

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Before diving into fixes — can you share what you’re ultimately trying to accomplish with this setup?

Are you trying to calculate milestone dates leading up to the webinar?

There may be a simpler or more reliable way to achieve it depending on your goal (especially with multi-level boards).

posts.author: Bernd01 (Bernd Hoffmann)
posts.cookedHtml:
The reason that your automation isn’t working correctly is because the automation doesn’t know what the “days” are that it needs to refrence. The “When webinar changes” is just a trigger, it triggers the automation to “push the date” which in turn, subtracts 25 days. Recipes can’t dynamically pull from other columns typically.
The only way to reference a new date when entered is to use monday’s workflows. However a workflow may not be able to reference layered subitems.
Using a workflow you can Set the trigger to be “When number changes” or “date changes” whatever you want your tigger to be, then set Webinar-date to “push date” dynamically, from the “Due Relative To” column.
That said, like Yogesh mentioned above, if you just need to know the date, for visual reference, you can just use a formula column that does math, so you can see the date. But you won’t be able to create automations referencing that date.

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Viva_la_Coaching
You can automate this correctly with Make automation so the math behaves exactly as expected.

A scenario would:

  • Trigger when the number field changes

  • Read the base date (your webinar date — 26 March)

  • Read the offset value (e.g., -25, +1, etc.)

  • Calculate: Base Date + Offset (positive or negative)

  • Update the Date column with the correct result
